domains.name: THREAT HUNTING — PEAK (Prepare · Execute · Act) + HMM

questions.text: As hipóteses são formuladas com o teste ABLE (Actor, Behavior, Location, Evidence).

levels.label: HMM0 · Inicial
levels.desc: Dependência exclusiva de alertas automáticos; pouca coleta de dados; sem capacidade de hunting.

levels.label: HMM1 · Mínimo
levels.desc: Incorpora inteligência de ameaças e faz buscas pontuais sobre coleta moderada de dados.

levels.label: HMM2 · Procedural
levels.desc: Executa regularmente procedimentos de hunting criados por terceiros, com boa coleta de dados.

levels.label: HMM3 · Inovador
levels.desc: Cria e documenta procedimentos próprios de hunting com técnicas analíticas variadas.

levels.label: HMM4 · Líder
levels.desc: Automatiza os procedimentos bem-sucedidos e concentra o esforço humano em inovação contínua.

questions.remark: PEAK recomenda o ABLE para garantir que a hipótese seja testável com os dados disponíveis.

questions.text: O escopo e o plano do hunt são definidos antes da execução (dados, janela, ativos-alvo).

questions.text: Have you identified the main business drivers?

levels.label: Limitado — Basic awareness of business drivers
Definido — Some business drivers have been identified
Gerenciado — Most business drivers have been identified
Otimizado — All business drivers are well known within the SOC

questions.remark: Example business drivers: cyber crime prevention, risk reduction, law / regulation, audit / compliance, business continuity

questions.text: Have you documented the main business drivers?

levels.label: Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Definido — Basic documentation of business drivers
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of business drivers
Otimizado — Document completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: Documentation of business drivers is important for demonstrable business alignment

questions.text: Do you use business drivers in the decision making process?

levels.label: Inicial — Business drivers are not part of decision making
Limitado — Business drivers are referred to on an ad-hoc basis
Definido — Business drivers are occasionally used in decisions
Gerenciado — Business drivers are used in most decisions
Otimizado — Business drivers are used in all relevant decisions

questions.remark: e.g. to determine priorities or make decisions regarding the on-boarding of new services or operations

questions.text: Do you regularly check if the current service catalogue is aligned with business drivers?

levels.label: Inicial — Service catalogue has not been checked for alignment
Limitado — Alignment is performed on an ad-hoc basis
Definido — Alignment was performed but not maintained
Gerenciado — Alignment is performed and maintained regularly
Otimizado — Every change in the catalogue is checked against drivers

questions.remark: i.e. do you check for services or operations that outside the scope of business drivers?

questions.text: Have the business drivers been validated with business stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — Business drivers have not been validated
Limitado — Basic awareness of SOC drivers exists among stakeholders
Definido — Stakeholders informally informed of business drivers
Gerenciado — Alignment of SOC drivers with stakeholders is performed
Otimizado — Business drivers are formally validated by stakeholders

questions.remark: Business stakeholders can be C-level management

questions.text: Have you identified the SOC customers & stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — SOC customers are not known
Limitado — Basic awareness of SOC customers
Definido — Some customers have been identified
Gerenciado — Customers have mostly been identified
Otimizado — All customers are identified, including relevance and context

questions.remark: Types of customers and stakeholders, their requirements, expectations, etc.

questions.text: Have you documented the main SOC customers & stakeholders?

levels.label: Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Definido — Basic documentation of SOC customers
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of SOC customers
Otimizado — Document completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: Formal registration of customer & stakeholder contact details, function, agreements, etc.

questions.text: Do you differentiate output towards these specific customers & stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — Output is the same for all customers
Definido — Some customers receive differentiated output
Gerenciado — All important customers receive differentiated output
Otimizado — All customers receive specific output based on context and type

questions.remark: Differentiating output is helpful to match expectations of customers & stakeholders

questions.text: Do you have service level agreements with these customers & stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — Contractual agreements not in place
Limitado — No contract in place, ad-hoc agreements made
Definido — Basic contract in place, not formally signed of
Gerenciado — Contract signed, but not regularly reviewed
Otimizado — Contract signed, approved by- and regularly reviewed with customers

questions.remark: Service level agreements are used to provide standardized services operating within known boundaries

questions.text: Do you regularly send updates to your customers & stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — No updates sent to customers
Limitado — Ad-hoc updates sent to some customers
Definido — Frequent updates sent to most customers
Gerenciado — Periodical updates sent to all customers
Otimizado — Periodical updates sent and discussed with all customers

questions.remark: For example: changes in service scope or delivery. Can also be reports, dashboards, etc.

questions.text: Do you actively measure and manage customer & stakeholder satisfaction?

levels.label: Inicial — Customer satisfaction not measured or managed
Limitado — Customer satisfaction managed in ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Customer satisfaction metrics defined, not applied structurally
Gerenciado — Customer satisfaction measured structurally, not actively managed
Otimizado — Customer satisfaction fully managed and improved over time

questions.remark: Understanding customer & stakeholder satisfaction will help to better align with business needs

questions.text: Does the SOC have a formal charter document in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No charter document in place
Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Gerenciado — Single charter, full description of SOC strategic elements
Otimizado — Charter completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: See 3.2 for charter document elements

questions.text: Is the SOC charter document regularly updated?

levels.label: Limitado — Charter is updated on ad-hoc basis
Definido — Charter is updated on major changes in business strategy
Otimizado — Charter periodically updated and realigned with business strategy

questions.remark: Regularity should be matched to your own internal policy. At least yearly is recommended

questions.text: Is the SOC charter document approved by the business / CISO?

levels.label: Limitado — Business / CISO has basic awareness of the charter
Definido — Business / CISO has full awareness of the charter
Gerenciado — Business / CISO approves of the content, but not formally
Otimizado — Charter is formally approved by the business / CISO

questions.remark: Approval from the relevant stakeholders will aid in business support for SOC operations

questions.text: Are all stakeholders familiar with the SOC charter document contents?

levels.label: Inicial — Stakeholders are unfamiliar with the charter
Limitado — Some stakeholders are aware of the charter, but not its contents
Definido — Some stakeholders are aware of the charter and its contents
Gerenciado — All stakeholders are aware, not all stakeholders know its contents
Otimizado — All Stakeholders are aware of the charter and its contents

questions.remark: Making stakeholders aware of the contents helps in getting organizational support for security operations

questions.text: Does the SOC have a governance process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — SOC governance process is not in place
Limitado — SOC governance is done in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Several governance elements are in place, but not structurally
Gerenciado — Formal governance process is in place that covers most SOC aspects
Otimizado — Formal governance process is in place and covers all SOC aspects

questions.remark: A governance process is required to determine the way the SOC should be managed

questions.text: Have all governance elements been identified?

levels.label: Inicial — No governance elements have been identified
Limitado — Some governance elements are identified and governed ad-hoc
Definido — Some governance elements are identified and governed actively
Gerenciado — Most governance elements are identified and actively governed
Otimizado — All elements are identified and actively governed

questions.remark: Possible governance elements can be found in under 4.3

questions.text: Is cost management in place?

levels.label: Inicial — Cost management not in place
Limitado — Costs visible, basic budget allocation in place
Definido — Costs fully visible and mostly managed, forecasting in place
Gerenciado — Costs fully managed, not formally aligned with business stakeholders
Otimizado — Costs fully managed and formally aligned with business stakeholders

questions.remark: Managing costs is required to justify budget allocation for the SOC and ensure continued service delivery in the future

questions.text: Is the governance process regularly reviewed?

levels.label: Inicial — Governance process is not reviewed
Limitado — Governance process is reviewed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Process is reviewed using a structured approach in an ad-hoc fashion
Gerenciado — Process is regularly and informally reviewed and updated
Otimizado — Process is regularly and formally reviewed and updated with findings

questions.remark: Frequency should be matched to your own internal policy. At least yearly is recommended

questions.text: Is the governance process aligned with all stakeholders?

levels.label: Inicial — Stakeholders are unfamiliar with the process
Limitado — Some stakeholders are aware of the process, but not its details
Definido — Some stakeholders are aware of the process and its details
Gerenciado — All stakeholders are aware, not all stakeholders know its details
Otimizado — All stakeholders are aware of the process and its details

questions.remark: Alignment will help the SOC obtain required mandate, budget and management support

questions.text: Is the SOC regularly audited or subjected to (external) assessments?

levels.label: Limitado — The SOC is assessed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — The SOC is assessed using a structured approach in an ad-hoc fashion
Gerenciado — The SOC is regularly and informally assessed
Otimizado — The SOC is regularly and formally assessed by a third party

questions.remark: Frequency should be matched to the SOC policy. At least yearly is recommended. 3rd party assessments have a higher objectivity

questions.text: Is there an active cooperation with other SOCs (external)?

levels.label: Inicial — No cooperation with other SOCs
Limitado — Some ad-hoc informal information exchange
Definido — Regular exchange with external SOCs, no defined process
Gerenciado — Defined process for information exchange, regular exchange in place
Otimizado — Continuous formalized active cooperation and information exchange

questions.remark: External collaboration and networking, exchaning best practices, threat intelligence, and response actions with other SOCs

questions.text: Is there an active cooperation with other SOCs (external)?

levels.label: Inicial — No cooperation with other SOCs
Limitado — Some ad-hoc informal information exchange
Definido — Regular exchange with external SOCs, no defined process
Gerenciado — Defined process for information exchange, regular exchange in place
Otimizado — Continuous formalized active cooperation and information exchange

questions.remark: External collaboration and networking, exchaning best practices, threat intelligence, and response actions with other SOCs

questions.text: Are SOC governance meetings regularly held?

levels.label: Limitado — Governance meetings held in an ad-hoc fashion
Gerenciado — Governance meetings at different levels scheduled and structured
Otimizado — Governance meetings at different levels scheduled, ToR formalised

questions.remark: Meetings at different levels (operational, tactical, strategic) should be formalised in Terms of Reference (ToR) and driven by metrics

questions.text: Is a privacy policy regarding security monitoring of employees in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No policy is in place
Limitado — Information regarding privacy is scattered across documents
Definido — A policy exists, but has not been accepted formally
Gerenciado — A formal policy exists, its contents are known to all employees
Otimizado — A formal policy exists, its contents are accepted by all employees

questions.remark: A privacy policy should state that monitoring of employees is possible within acceptable limits

questions.text: Does the SOC operate in compliance with all applicable privacy laws and regulations?

levels.label: Inicial — Regulations are not known and the SOC is non-compliant
Limitado — Some regulations are known and the SOC is non-compliant
Definido — Most regulations are known and the SOC is partially compliant
Gerenciado — Regulations are fully known and the SOC is mostly compliant
Otimizado — Regulations are fully known and the SOC is fully compliant

questions.remark: Local laws and regulations as well as company policy may apply and should all be considered

questions.text: Does the SOC cooperate with legal departments regarding privacy matters?

levels.label: Inicial — There is no cooperation between the SOC and legal
Limitado — There is some ad-hoc cooperation between SOC and legal
Definido — There is structural cooperation between SOC and legal
Gerenciado — Alignment exists between SOC and legal
Otimizado — Full and regular alignment exists between SOC and legal

questions.remark: Cooperation will ensure that the SOC is enabled to perform activities, rather than blocked

questions.text: Are specific procedures in place for dealing with privacy related investigations?

levels.label: Inicial — No privacy procedures in place
Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of privacy investigations
Otimizado — Procedure completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: Privacy related issues require careful examination, especially those potentially leading to court cases

questions.text: Specify rationale for chosen values or any additional comments

levels.label: Inicial — The SOC is unaware of any information
Limitado — The SOC is aware of such information, no formal identification
Definido — The SOC is fully aware, some information is formally identified
Gerenciado — Most privacy related information is identified and documented
Otimizado — All privacy related information is identified and documented

questions.remark: Such information includes IP addresses, customer identifiers, user names, host names (for personally owned devices), etc.

questions.text: Is a Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) regularly conducted?

levels.label: Limitado — PIAs are conducted in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — PIAs are conducted using a structured approach in an ad-hoc fashion
Gerenciado — PIAs are conducted informally and regularly
Otimizado — PIAs are conducted formally and regularly

questions.remark: Can be used to determine the impact of monitoring on privacy, and can help uncover potential violations

questions.text: Is there an information security policy in place that supports the SOC activities?

levels.label: Inicial — No formal information security policy in place
Limitado — Information security policy in place, no mention of SOC activities
Definido — Policy in place, SOC activities mentioned without mandate
Gerenciado — Policy in place, SOC activities mentioned in detail with mandate
Otimizado — Policy with SOC activities and mandate in place, actively communicated

questions.remark: A clear security policy that supports SOC operations provides guidance and helps to enforce mandate for the SOC in the organisation

questions.text: Is the SOC consulted in the creation and updates of operational security policy?

levels.label: Inicial — SOC not consulted or informed of policy creation or policy updates
Limitado — SOC informed of policy creation and updates only
Definido — SOC consulted before policy creation and updates, not actively involved
Gerenciado — SOC consulted before policy creation and updates, performs reviews
Otimizado — Full involvement of the SOC in the creation of operational security policy

questions.remark: Consulting the SOC in the creation of security policy will ensure that SOC activities are properly mentioned and enforceable

questions.text: Is a reporting policy for security incidents in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No reporting policy in place
Limitado — Policy in place, no mention of the SOC
Definido — Policy in place, role of the SOC in security incidents mentioned
Gerenciado — Policy in place with all SOC activities, no central point for reporting
Otimizado — Policy & central point for reporting in place, SOC part of the workflow

questions.remark: A reporting policy for security incidents will aid the SOC in identifying incidents and threats in the organization

questions.text: Has a SOC policy been created?

levels.label: Inicial — No SOC policy in place
Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Definido — Basic SOC policy document created
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of SOC policy elements
Otimizado — SOC policy document completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: A SOC policy is to outline the rules to which SOC personnel and SOC management needs to adhere

questions.text: Does the current size of the SOC meet FTE requirements?

levels.label: Inicial — The SOC is either heavily overstaffed or understaffed
Limitado — The SOC is overstaffed or understaffed
Definido — The SOC is somewhat overstaffed or understaffed
Gerenciado — The SOC mostly meets FTE requirements
Otimizado — The SOC is staffed to full satisfaction in terms of FTE requirements

questions.remark: i.e. is the SOC size sufficient to realize business goals?

questions.text: Does the SOC meet requirements for internal to external employee FTE ratio?

levels.label: Inicial — There are either way too few or too many external employees
Limitado — There are too few or too many external employees
Definido — The SOC has somewhat too many or too few external employees
Gerenciado — The SOC mostly meets requirements for external employee FTE count
Otimizado — The external employee ratio meets all requirements

questions.remark: Note: requirements do not need to be explicit. Set importance to 'None' if you have no external employees.

questions.text: Does the SOC meet requirements for internal to external employee skillset?

levels.label: Inicial — There are too many skills only present within the external employees
Limitado — Some required skills are not present internally, and not transferred
Definido — Some required skills are not present internally, but being transferred
Gerenciado — Most skills are covered with internal employees
Otimizado — All required skills are covered with internal employees as well

questions.remark: i.e. Are there any crucial skills amongst external employees? Set importance to 'None' if you have no external employees

questions.text: Are all positions filled?

levels.label: Inicial — Not all positions filled, service delivery cannot be assured
Limitado — Sufficient positions filled to ensure service delivery
Gerenciado — All positions currently filled, not meeting external ration requirements
Otimizado — All positions currently filled, meeting external ratio requirements

questions.remark: Unfilled positions may be due to deficiencies in the recruitment process

questions.text: Do you have a recruitment process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — There is no recruitment process in place
Limitado — Recruitment is performed at an ad-hoc basis
Definido — A basic recruitment process is in place
Gerenciado — A full recruitment process is in place, but not performing effectively
Otimizado — A full recruitment process is in place and performing effectively

questions.remark: A recruitment process is required to obtain new employees in a market where talent is scarce

questions.text: Do you have a talent acquisition process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No talent acquisition process in place
Limitado — Talent acquisition is performed at an ad-hoc basis
Definido — A basic talent acquisition process is in place
Gerenciado — A full acquisition process is in place, but not performing effectively
Otimizado — A full acquisition process is in place and performing effectively

questions.remark: Talent sourcing can be vital for SOC success

questions.text: Do you have specific KSAOs established for SOC personnel?

levels.label: Inicial — KSAOs have not been created
Limitado — KSOAs are used ad-hoc in staffing activities
Definido — A basic standardized KSAO set is created
Gerenciado — A full KSAO is created, but not actively used in staffing
Otimizado — A full KSAO is created, regularly updated and actively used in staffing

questions.remark: Knowledge, Skills, Abilities and Other attributes (KSAOs) should be in place: technical, cognitive, social and character

questions.text: Do you actively seek to create a psychologically safe environment for SOC personnel?

levels.label: Inicial — A safe environment is not actively created
Limitado — Basic awareness of a safe environment exists, but is not implemented
Definido — A safe environment has been established, but not actively managed
Gerenciado — A safe environment has been established, and actively managed
Otimizado — A safe and actively managed environment exists and is evaluated

questions.remark: A psychologically safe environment is an environment where everyone is able to speak their mind and feel valued

questions.text: Do you have a defined retention strategy?
questions.remark: A retention strategy is used to ensure that talent and key personnel is not lost

questions.text: Do you formally differentiate roles within the SOC?

levels.label: Inicial — No roles are used in the SOC
Limitado — Some roles exist, but are not actively being used
Definido — Some roles exist, and are actively being used
Gerenciado — All roles are fully in use, but not formalized
Otimizado — All roles are fully in use and formalized

questions.remark: Use the roles in 2.2 to determine if you have all roles required in the SOC

questions.text: Do you differentiate tiers within these roles?

levels.label: Inicial — No tiers exist within these roles
Limitado — Some tiers exist, but are not actively being used
Definido — Some tiers exist, and are actively being used
Gerenciado — All tiers are fully in use, but not formalized
Otimizado — All relevant roles are tiered and formalized

questions.remark: If you have no tiers, and you feel this is not a restriction, select importance 'None'

questions.text: Are all roles sufficiently staffed?

levels.label: Inicial — None of the roles meets FTE requirements
Limitado — Some roles meet FTE requirements
Definido — Vital roles meet FTE requirements
Gerenciado — All vital roles and most other roles meet FTE requirements
Otimizado — All roles fully meet FTE requirements

questions.remark: Consider the staffing levels (desired FTE count) as well as knowledge and experience for all roles

questions.text: Is there a role-based hierarchy in your SOC?

levels.label: Limitado — A basic hierarchy exists, but is not fully operational
Definido — A basic hierarchy is in place and fully operational
Gerenciado — A full hierarchy is in place, but not formalized
Otimizado — A full hierarchy is in place and formalized

questions.remark: If you have no hierarchy, and you feel this is not a restriction, select importance 'None'

questions.text: Have you formally documented all SOC roles?

levels.label: Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Definido — Basic documentation of SOC roles
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of SOC roles
Otimizado — Document completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: Possible documentation elements can be found in under 2.7

questions.text: Are responsibilities for each role understood?

levels.label: Definido — Responsibilities for some roles understood and adhered to
Gerenciado — Responsibilities for all roles mostly understood and adhered to
Otimizado — Full understanding of responsibilities formalized in training sessions

questions.remark: Responsibilities for each role should be clearly understood by all SOC personnel

questions.text: Have you documented career progression requirements for each of these roles?

levels.label: Limitado — Some ad-hoc information across documents
Definido — Basic documentation of career progression for roles
Gerenciado — Single document, full description of career progression for roles
Otimizado — Document completed, approved and formally published

questions.remark: Career progression for roles can be documented through training, certification, experience and soft skills requirements

questions.text: Do you regularly revise or update the role descriptions?

levels.label: Limitado — Documentation is reviewed ad-hoc, not using a structured approach
Definido — Documentation is reviewed ad-hoc, using a structured approach
Gerenciado — Documentation is regularly and informally reviewed and updated
Otimizado — Documentation is regularly and formally reviewed and updated

questions.remark: To revise is to review and verify whether to documentation is still correct or requires an update

questions.text: Do you have a job rotation plan in place?

levels.label: Limitado — A plan covering some roles is in place, but not operational
Definido — A plan covering some roles is in place and operational
Gerenciado — A plan covering all roles is in place, but not formalized
Otimizado — A plan covering all roles is in place and formalized

questions.remark: Job rotation can be used to train employees in a variety of tasks and avoid too much routine

questions.text: Do you have a career progression process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No career progression process is in place
Limitado — A process covering some roles is in place, but not operational
Definido — A process covering some roles is in place and operational
Gerenciado — A process covering all roles is in place, but not formalized
Otimizado — A process covering all roles is in place and formalized

questions.remark: Career development, promotion, etc.

questions.text: Do you have a talent management process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No talent management process in place
Limitado — Talent management is performed at an ad-hoc basis
Definido — A basic talent management process is in place
Gerenciado — A full process is in place, but not performing effectively
Otimizado — A full talent management process is in place and performing effectively

questions.remark: Talent should be adequately managed to retain such staff and fully develop their potential.

questions.text: Do you have team diversity goals?

levels.label: Limitado — Diversity goals are recognized but not defined
Definido — Diversity goals are defined but not formalized
Gerenciado — Diversity goals have been formally defined and are not met
Otimizado — Diversity goals have been formally defined and are met

questions.remark: e.g. background diversity, ethnic diversity, gender diversity, etc.

questions.text: Do you periodically evaluate SOC employees?

levels.label: Inicial — No periodic evaluation is performed
Limitado — Periodic evaluation is performed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Periodic evaluation is performed in a structured fashion
Gerenciado — Periodic evaluation is performed, but results are not used structurally
Otimizado — Periodic evaluation is performed, results are used for personal growth

questions.remark: Can also be included  in the regular organization evaluation process

questions.text: Do you have a 'new hire' process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — No new hire process in place
Limitado — New hire training is done in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — A process is in place, but does not cover all aspects
Gerenciado — An informal process covering people, process and technology is in place
Otimizado — A formal process covering people, process and technology is in place

questions.remark: i.e. a defined process to quickly let new employees find their place and perform well in the SOC

questions.text: Are all SOC employees subjected to screening?

levels.label: Limitado — Basic screening performed in ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Basic screening procedure in place, applied structurally
Gerenciado — Full screening procedure in place, applied structurally, not formalized
Otimizado — Formal screening procedure and background checks applied structurally

questions.remark: Personnel screening is performed to avoid infiltration or misbehaviour by SOC employees

questions.text: Do you measure employee satisfaction for improving the SOC?

levels.label: Inicial — Employee satisfaction is not measured
Limitado — Employee satisfaction is measured in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Satisfaction is usually measured, but not embedded in processes
Gerenciado — Employee satisfaction is measured, not used for improvement
Otimizado — Employee satisfaction measured periodically and used for improvement

questions.remark: Employee satisfaction should be taken seriously as lack of satisfaction may lead to key personnel leaving

questions.text: Are there regular 1-on-1 meetings between the SOC manager and the employees?

levels.label: Inicial — 1-on-1 meetings are not held within the SOC
Limitado — 1-on-1 meetings are held on ad-hoc basis
Definido — Informal 1-on-1 meetings are held periodically
Gerenciado — Formal 1-on-1 meetings are regularly held, results are not structured
Otimizado — 1-on-1 meetings are regularly held and used for coaching and growth

questions.remark: Such informal 1-on-1 conversations are used to coach employees and help the SOC manager gain insight in personal challenges

questions.text: Do you perform regular teambuilding exercises?

levels.label: Inicial — No team building exercises are performed
Limitado — Exercises are performed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Exercises are usually performed, but not embedded in processes
Gerenciado — Exercises are regularly done, but not focused on improvement
Otimizado — Exercises are regularly done and focused on improving team dynamics

questions.remark: Teambuilding exercises are used to promote collaboration between individuals in the team and to raise team spirit

questions.text: Have you established team goals?

levels.label: Inicial — Team goals are not determined
Limitado — Team goals are determined, but not formally documented
Definido — Team goals are determined, and formally documented
Gerenciado — Team goals are determined and documented, but not tracked
Otimizado — Team goals are determined, approved and tracked regularly

questions.remark: Team goals help to bring focus to the team and monitor progress

questions.text: Do you document and track individual team member goals?

levels.label: Inicial — Individual goals are not determined
Limitado — Individual goals are determined, but not formally documented
Definido — Individual goals are determined, and formally documented
Gerenciado — Individual goals are determined and documented, but not tracked
Otimizado — Individual goals are determined, approved and tracked regularly

questions.remark: Individual team member goals should be set to help grow the employee to full potential

questions.text: Do you perform regular teambuilding exercises with other teams relevant to the SOC?

levels.label: Inicial — No MTS team building exercises are performed
Limitado — MTS exercises are performed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — MTS exercises are usually performed, but not embedded in processes
Gerenciado — MTS exercises are regularly done, but not focused on improvement
Otimizado — MTS exercises done regularly and focused on improving team dynamics

questions.remark: In multi-team systems (MTS), the SOC collaborates with other teams. Use cross-team teambuilding to maximize performance

questions.text: Do you periodically evaluate team performance?

levels.label: Inicial — No periodic evaluation is performed
Limitado — Periodic evaluation is performed in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Periodic evaluation is performed in a structured fashion
Gerenciado — Periodic evaluation is performed, but results are not used structurally
Otimizado — Periodic evaluation is performed, results are used for team growth

questions.remark: Besides individual performance, team performance and dynamics are also important to measure and improve on

questions.text: Do you have a formal knowledge management process in place?

levels.label: Inicial — A knowledge management process is not in place
Limitado — Knowledge management is done in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — A basic process is in place, that covers some knowledge aspects
Gerenciado — An informal process is in place that covers most knowledge aspects
Otimizado — A formal process is in place, covering all knowledge aspects

questions.remark: Formal knowledge management helps to optimize knowledge creation and distribution

questions.text: Do you regularly assess and revise the knowledge management process?

levels.label: Limitado — Documentation is reviewed ad-hoc, not using a structured approach
Definido — Documentation is reviewed ad-hoc, using a structured approach
Gerenciado — Documentation is regularly and informally reviewed and updated
Otimizado — Documentation is regularly and formally reviewed and updated

questions.remark: This refers to the knowledge management process as a whole

questions.text: Is there effective tooling in place to support knowledge documentation and distribution?

levels.label: Inicial — Tooling is not in place
Limitado — Tooling is in place, but used in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Tooling is in place, and used regularly
Gerenciado — Tooling is in place and use of the tool is embedded in processes
Otimizado — Tooling is in place and optimized for knowledge management purposes

questions.remark: Such tooling can help to avoid investigation similar issues multiple times by integrating into the security monitoring process

questions.text: Have you documented SOC team member abilities?

levels.label: Inicial — Documentation is not in place
Limitado — Documentation only covers some employees' abilities
Definido — Documentation covers the most relevant abilities for the team
Gerenciado — All employee abilities documented, but is not regularly updated
Otimizado — All employee abilities documented, and regularly updated

questions.remark: Besides knowledge and skills, team member abilities are also important to document

questions.text: Do you have a skill matrix in place?

levels.label: Inicial — A skill matrix is not in place
Limitado — A basic skill matrix is in place, but incomplete
Definido — A complete skill matrix is in place, not approved
Gerenciado — A complete skill matrix is in place and approved, not regularly updated
Otimizado — A complete skill matrix is in place, approved and regularly updated

questions.remark: A matrix may consist of: SOC skills, SOC employees and skill levels (novice, intermediate, expert)

questions.text: Is the skill matrix actively used for team and personal improvement?

levels.label: Inicial — Matrix not used for improvement
Limitado — Matrix used for improvement in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Matrix used to improve some personal and team results
Gerenciado — Matrix used to improve all personal and team results
Otimizado — Matrix used to improve personal and team results, improvements tracked

questions.remark: Personal improvement is essential, team improvement requires insight in team dynamics and skill distribution

questions.text: Do you have a knowledge matrix in place?

levels.label: Inicial — A knowledge matrix is not in place
Limitado — A basic knowledge matrix is in place, but incomplete
Definido — A complete knowledge matrix is in place, not approved
Gerenciado — A complete knowledge matrix is in place and approved, not regularly updated
Otimizado — A complete knowledge matrix is in place, approved and regularly updated

questions.text: Is the knowledge matrix actively used to determine training and education needs?

levels.label: Inicial — Matrix not used for identification of training needs
Limitado — Matrix used for training identification in an ad-hoc fashion
Definido — Matrix used to identify training needs, but not for all employees
Gerenciado — Matrix used to identify all training needs, but not tracked for execution
Otimizado — Matrix structurally used to identify training needs, training tracked

questions.remark: The matrix should be used as a means to identify and resolve knowledge gaps

questions.text: Do you have a training program in place?

levels.label: Inicial — A training program is not in place
Limitado — A training program some roles is in place, but not operational
Definido — A training program covering some roles is in place and operational
Gerenciado — A training program covering all roles is in place, but not formalized
Otimizado — A training program covering all roles is in place and formalized

questions.remark: A training program is used to ensure a minimal level of knowledge for employees

questions.text: Do you have a certification program in place?

levels.label: Inicial — A certification program is not in place
Limitado — A certification program some roles is in place, but not operational
Definido — A certification program covering some roles is in place and operational
Gerenciado — A certification program covering all roles is in place, but not formalized
Otimizado — A certification program covering all roles is in place and formalized

questions.remark: A certification program is used to provide a demonstrable minimum level of knowledge and skills

questions.text: Is the training and certification program connected to evaluation and career progression?

levels.label: Inicial — The programs are not connected

Definido — The programs are regularly used, but not embedded in processes
Gerenciado — The programs are mostly aligned, but not formally
Otimizado — The programs are formally embedded in evaluation and progression

questions.remark: e.g. certain training and certifications are required to grow from a junior level function to a more senior level function

questions.text: Is there a reserved budget for education and training?

levels.label: Limitado — Insufficient budget is allocated for the team as a whole
Definido — Sufficient budget is allocated for the team as a whole
Gerenciado — Employees have sufficient budget, not encouraged to attend training
Otimizado — Employees have sufficient budget, encouraged to attend training

questions.remark: i.e. a fixed percentage of the total SOC budget that is allocated for education and cannot be used for other purposes

questions.text: Is there a reserved amount of time for education and training?

levels.label: Limitado — Insufficient time is allocated for the team as a whole
Definido — Sufficient time is allocated for the team as a whole
Gerenciado — Employees have sufficient time, but not encouraged to attend training
Otimizado — Employees have sufficient time, and encouraged to attend training

questions.remark: This is an extension of education budget

questions.text: Do you have regular workshops for knowledge development?

levels.label: Limitado — Workshops are held in an ad-hoc fashion
Gerenciado — Workshops are held regularly, not aligned with knowledge & training
Otimizado — Workshops are held regularly and aligned with knowledge & training

questions.text: Is there a use case management process or framework in place?

questions.text: Are use cases formally documented?

questions.remark: e.g. use cases can be derived from business requirements, risk assessments, threat management / intelligence

questions.text: Hash value hunting
questions.remark: 'Trivial', lowest added value as these change swiftly

questions.text: IP address hunting
questions.remark: 'Easy', low added value

questions.text: Domain name hunting
questions.remark: 'Simple', somewhat higher added value

questions.text: Network artefact hunting
questions.remark: 'Annoying', include network flow, packet capture, proxy logs, active network connections, historic connections, ports and services

questions.text: Host-based artefact hunting
questions.remark: 'Annoying', includes users, processes, services, drivers, files, registry, hardware, memory, disk activity, network connections, etc.

questions.text: Adversary tools hunting
questions.remark: 'Challenging', includes dual-use tools

questions.text: Adversary TTP hunting
questions.remark: 'Tough!', requires detailed knowledge of adversaries and their modus operandi

questions.text: Inbound threat hunting
questions.remark: Hunting for inbound threats such as inbound connections, DNS zone transfers, inbound emails

questions.text: Outbound threat hunting
questions.remark: Hunting for outbound threats such as C&C traffic, outbound emails

questions.text: Internal threat hunting
questions.remark: Hunting for threats inside the organization. Hunting may focus on lateral movement or anomalous network connections

questions.text: Outlier detection
questions.remark: Using statistical methods to detect outliers, such as least frequency of occurrence

questions.text: Hunting coverage
questions.remark: How well does the hunting process cover your environment? All assets & network traffic, or only partially? Scalability is key

questions.text: Leveraging of existing tooling
questions.remark: Existing tools may include the SIEM system, firewall analysis tools, etc.

questions.text: Custom hunting scripts and tools
questions.remark: Any custom scripts to assist the hunting process. May include scripts to scan end-points for particular artifacts

questions.text: Dedicated hunting platform
questions.remark: Dedicated tooling for the hunting process

questions.text: Continuous hunting data collection
questions.remark: Continuous collection of information can be used to alert on indicators and to preserve system state

questions.text: Historic hunting
questions.remark: Hunting for indicators that may have been present on end-points in the past. Requires some sort of saved state

questions.text: Automated hunting
questions.remark: Fully automated hunting capability

questions.text: Hunt alerting
questions.remark: Automated alerting based on queries performed in the hunting process

questions.text: Vulnerability information integration
questions.remark: Integration of vulnerability information into the hunting process to provide additional context

questions.text: Threat intelligence integration
questions.remark: Integration of threat intelligence information into the hunting process, threat hunts should be driven by threat intelligence
